div1.entryFree:
Maliciously, 1) malignantly: Cor. I, 1, 35.
2) like one full of hate, with the strength of hate: I will be treble-sinewed, hearted, breathed, and fight m. Ant. III, 13, 179. Used of strong poison: a lingering dram that should not work m. like poison, Wint. I, 2, 321 (in an apparently pernicious manner).
